issues.description: Email regex is too permissive and may allow invalid email formats (e.g., multiple @ symbols, invalid TLDs)
issues.suggestion: Use a more robust email validation library like validator.js or implement RFC 5322 compliant regex

issues.description: HTML entity encoding is incomplete and may not prevent all XSS attacks (missing & and other special characters)
issues.suggestion: Use a dedicated sanitization library like DOMPurify or he.js for comprehensive HTML escaping

issues.description: Email is not sanitized before storage, only name is sanitized
issues.suggestion: Consider sanitizing or validating email input more thoroughly before storage

issues.description: ID generation is not guaranteed to be unique - relies on timestamp and random string which could collide in concurrent operations
issues.suggestion: Use UUID v4 (crypto.randomUUID() or uuid library) for guaranteed unique identifiers

issues.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and predictable IDs could be exploited
issues.suggestion: Use crypto.randomUUID() or crypto.getRandomValues() for secure random ID generation

issues.description: Database query is commented out - actual implementation is missing
issues.suggestion: Implement actual database connection or add TODO comment explaining this is a stub

issues.description: Email toLowerCase() could fail with null/undefined if validation is bypassed
issues.suggestion: Add null/undefined check or use optional chaining: input.email?.toLowerCase()

issues.description: Empty string check is loose - whitespace-only strings would pass validation
Function accepts string but doesn't handle null/undefined cases
issues.suggestion: Add type guard or make parameter optional: (input: string | null | undefined): string

codeQuality.summary: Generally well-structured code with good validation practices, but has moderate security concerns with ID generation and incomplete input sanitization that should be addressed.

untestedPaths.reasoning: Email validation is a security-critical function that determines if user input is accepted. Incorrect validation could allow malformed emails or reject valid ones.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test with valid emails (user@example.com, user.name+tag@example.co.uk), invalid emails (missing @, missing domain, spaces, special chars), and edge cases (empty string, very long email, unicode characters)

untestedPaths.reasoning: XSS prevention function is security-critical. Missing tests could leave application vulnerable to cross-site scripting attacks.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test that all dangerous HTML characters are escaped:

untestedPaths.reasoning: Main user creation function handles validation, sanitization, and data persistence. This is a public API function with multiple execution paths.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test successful user creation with valid input, verify email is lowercased, name is sanitized, and ID is generated. Test error cases: missing email, missing name, invalid email format. Verify async database save is called with correct parameters.

untestedPaths.reasoning: Error handling branch that prevents user creation when email is missing needs explicit testing to ensure proper validation.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Call createUser with empty string email and with undefined email, verify both throw 'Email and name are required' error

untestedPaths.reasoning: Error handling branch that prevents user creation when name is missing needs explicit testing.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Call createUser with empty string name and with undefined name, verify both throw 'Email and name are required' error

untestedPaths.reasoning: Error handling for malformed email addresses must be tested to ensure invalid emails are rejected.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Call createUser with invalid email formats (no @, no domain, spaces), verify each throws 'Invalid email format' error

untestedPaths.reasoning: Email lowercasing is important for preventing duplicate accounts with different casing. Must verify this transformation occurs.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Create user with email 'USER@EXAMPLE.COM', verify returned user object has email 'user@example.com'

untestedPaths.reasoning: ID generation function should produce unique, properly formatted IDs. While internal, it's crucial for data integrity.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Call generateId multiple times in sequence, verify each ID starts with 'user_', contains timestamp and random string, and all generated IDs are unique

untestedPaths.reasoning: Database persistence function uses parameterized queries for SQL injection protection. Must verify query structure and parameter order.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Mock database query function, call saveUser with user object, verify db.query called with parameterized INSERT statement and parameters in correct order [id, email, name, createdAt]

untestedPaths.reasoning: Real-world XSS attempts often combine multiple attack vectors. Must test complex input strings.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Pass string containing mix of <, >, ", and ' characters like '<script>alert("XSS")</script>', verify all are escaped to '<script>alert("XSS")</script>'

untestedPaths.reasoning: If saveUser fails (database error), createUser should propagate the error properly.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Mock saveUser to reject with database error, call createUser with valid input, verify promise rejects with the database error

untestedPaths.reasoning: Email regex may have edge cases with international domains, subdomains, or special valid formats.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test edge case emails: very long local part (64 chars), multiple subdomains (a@b.c.d.example.com), plus addressing (user+tag@example.com), consecutive dots, starting/ending with dots

untestedPaths.reasoning: Function should handle edge cases like empty strings, very long strings, and strings with no dangerous characters.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test with empty string (returns empty), normal string with no special chars (returns unchanged), very long string with special chars (all escaped), string with only special chars

untestedPaths.reasoning: Input with only whitespace or leading/trailing whitespace may cause issues if not handled.
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Call createUser with name that is only whitespace, with email that has leading/trailing spaces - verify behavior is consistent and appropriate

testCoverage.summary: No test file exists for fixtures/clean-code.ts - all 5 exported functions (isValidEmail, sanitizeInput, createUser, generateId, saveUser) and multiple critical security-related code paths are completely untested.

suggestions.description: Replace timestamp-based ID generation with crypto.randomUUID() for better uniqueness guarantees and standard compliance
suggestions.benefits: Uses standard UUID v4 format, cryptographically secure random values, better collision resistance, and eliminates timestamp dependency

suggestions.description: Extract input validation logic into a dedicated validateUserInput function for better separation of concerns and reusability
suggestions.benefits: Improves testability, makes validation logic reusable, reduces createUser function complexity, and follows single responsibility principle

suggestions.description: Use more comprehensive email validation or reference RFC 5322 standard, current regex is overly simplistic
suggestions.benefits: More accurate email validation, rejects invalid formats like 'a@b.', handles special characters correctly, reduces false positives

suggestions.description: Use object mapping for HTML entity replacements to reduce repetitive replace calls
suggestions.benefits: More maintainable, easier to add new entities, single regex pass instead of four, better performance for large strings

suggestions.description: Apply builder pattern or use a UserFactory class to encapsulate user creation logic and improve testability
suggestions.benefits: Better dependency injection for testing, easier to mock dependencies, clearer separation of concerns, follows SOLID principles, improves testability dramatically

suggestions.description: Sanitize email as well to prevent potential injection attacks through email field
suggestions.benefits: Consistent input sanitization across all user-provided fields, better defense-in-depth security posture

suggestions.description: Add early return pattern and more specific error messages for better debugging
suggestions.benefits: More specific error messages help with debugging, clearer validation logic, better user experience

refactorings.summary: Identified 7 refactoring opportunities including modernizing ID generation with crypto.randomUUID, extracting validation logic, applying factory pattern for better testability, and improving sanitization consistency.

recommendations.description: Add comprehensive test suite covering all security-critical functions (isValidEmail, sanitizeInput, createUser) with edge cases and error paths
Replace Math.random() based ID generation with crypto.randomUUID() to ensure unique, cryptographically secure identifiers and prevent potential ID collision or prediction attacks
Enhance input sanitization by using a dedicated library (DOMPurify or he.js) to ensure comprehensive XSS protection beyond basic HTML entity encoding
Improve email validation with RFC 5322 compliant regex or use a validation library to handle edge cases and prevent invalid email formats from passing validation
Refactor createUser to use factory pattern with dependency injection to improve testability and separation of concerns
Add whitespace trimming to input validation and provide more specific error messages for better user feedback
